summary: First test run for JAM Academy LMS. Completed backend pytest suite (26/26 passed) covering auth, courses CRUD + enroll + progress, tests (creation/attempt/role hiding), live classes, assignments (create/submit/grade), announcements, dashboards and role enforcement (403s). Ran full Playwright e2e for both student and teacher personas (18/18 flows passed) — register, login, invalid-login error, protected-route redirect, enroll, mark lesson complete, take test with timer + scorecard + attempted state, submit assignment, view live classes/announcements, teacher dashboard, create course + section + lesson, create test + view results, schedule + delete live class, create assignment + grade submission, post + delete announcement, fresh student sees graded 8/10 with feedback. No issues detected.
critical_code_review_comments: Server bind uses on_event startup which is deprecated in FastAPI (works fine, but consider lifespan). Non-blocking.

CORS_ORIGINS default is '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers ignore credentials when origin is *, so setting a real origin list is advisable for production (current stack works because frontend uses Bearer token, not cookies).
auth_utils.get_current_user pops password_hash but returns user dict directly from Motor; safe here because docs are simple. Consider a shared Pydantic response model.

Enrollment/attempt duplicate prevention uses find_one + insert; the unique index on enrollments (course_id, student_id) helps but there's no unique index on test_attempts (test_id, student_id) — small race-condition window if two POSTs are fired simultaneously. Recommend adding a unique compound index.
test_credentials: Teacher: teacher@jamacademy.com / Teacher@123 · Student: student@jamacademy.com / Student@123 · Also registered fresh UI-test students dynamically (stu_ui_<ts>@example.com / Passw0rd!).
seed_data_creation: Backend seeded on startup (4 courses, 2 mock tests, 3 live classes, 2 assignments, 3 announcements). Tests created their own TEST_-prefixed data and cleaned up where possible; a few UITEST courses/tests/assignments remain in DB from the Playwright run (they don't affect existing flows).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All critical happy-path and role-enforcement flows are green. The Playwright e2e adds UITEST-prefixed leftovers (a course, a test, an assignment) — safe to ignore or clean up. Fresh student registration is required for the take-test flow because attempts are one-time; the seeded student may already have attempted a test after a prior run. All data-testids used in the request are present and correct. Backend uses JWT Bearer via localStorage 'jam_token' (also sets httpOnly cookie 'access_token' as fallback). No mocked APIs.
